title: Neuroloma australe

Neuroloma australe is a member of the order Lamiales and is closely related to other plants in the same family. This plant is further classified into the subclass Asteridae and the clade Angiosperms. Neuroloma australe is a unique species with specific characteristics that distinguish it from other plants in its classification.

Neurolaena australe is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a weed in some regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Neurolaena australe include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neurolaena australe is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
